mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase discount rates applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically depressing S&P 500 valuations and generating negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate above 4.0% signals deteriorating consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings pressure, driving negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns remain positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.0%, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio falls below 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and construction activity, boosting corporate revenue expectations and equity prices.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 20-day returns in >60% of cases, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production below its 12-month moving average reflects weakening manufacturing output and supply chain bottlenecks, triggering short-term equity sell-offs.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >70% of periods when INDPRO falls below its 12-month average during tightening cycles, or predictive accuracy drops below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation combined with stable or falling interest rates erodes real yields and signals loss of central bank credibility, causing equity markets to price in future aggressive tightening and negative returns.
failure_condition: The joint condition yields positive 60-day returns in >58% of historical instances, or the interaction effect vanishes in multivariate regression (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands household income and consumer spending, directly supporting corporate earnings and generating positive equity returns.
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS growth correlates with negative 20-day returns in >60% of months, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.01 over a 5-year evaluation period.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth falling below 1.0% indicates economic stagnation that reduces corporate profit margins and equity valuations over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in >65% of quarters where GDP growth is below 1.0%, or out-of-sample R-squared remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising unemployment and falling industrial production confirms a broad economic slowdown, prompting institutional investors to reduce equity allocations and driving short-term negative returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases and INDPRO decreases simultaneously
failure_condition: The divergence signal yields positive 5-day returns in >62% of cases, or the joint predictor fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark in walk-forward testing.
